user_message: Just got this from Andrew Taylor at Pacific Northwest Outfitters -- I need help figuring out executive brief. Renewal conversation is tomorrow.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Andrew Taylor <andrew.taylor@pacificnorth.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-10 08:50 EST
Subject: executive brief - Action Required

Just checking in on the executive brief proposal. Our IT Director is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
To: Andrew Taylor <andrew.taylor@pacificnorth.com>
Date: 2024-02-05 17:02 EST
Subject: Re: executive brief Discussion - Next Steps

There's been a change in our timeline for executive brief. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Pacific Northwest Outfitters: the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF37-B trap (Over-escalates a routine service complaint); produces complete Severity triage, root-cause hypothesis (labeled as hypothesis), customer response draft, ACR risk fl
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF37-B failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF37-B failure mode (Over-escalates a routine service complaint)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct executive brief decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF37-B -- Over-escalates a routine service complaint
